findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name User-controlled FlippingStrategy class name loaded via Class.forName
 * @description HTTP request input flows to Class.forName() to instantiate FlippingStrategy - arbitrary class loading
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/ff4j-flipping-strategy-class-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module FlippingStrategyClassInjectionConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getArgument(0) and
      (
        mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%lipping%") or
        mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName().matches("%trategy%") or
        mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Mapping%") or
        mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Console%") or
        mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Feature%")
      )
    )
  }
}

module FlippingStrategyClassInjectionFlow = TaintTracking::Global<FlippingStrategyClassInjectionConfig>;
import FlippingStrategyClassInjectionFlow::PathGraph

from FlippingStrategyClassInjectionFlow::PathNode source, FlippingStrategyClassInjectionFlow::PathNode sink
where FlippingStrategyClassInjectionFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "User-controlled class name flows to Class.forName() for FlippingStrategy instantiation from $@.", source.getNode(), "user input"
findings.hypothesis: User-controlled FlippingStrategy class name from HTTP request flows to Class.forName() enabling arbitrary class loading

User-controlled class name flows to Class.forName() for FlippingStrategy instantiation from [user input](1).
User-controlled class name flows to Class.forName() for FlippingStrategy instantiation from [user input](2).

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name User input to PropertyClass type - HTTP API class injection via property type field
 * @description The HTTP REST API accepts a property type field (className) which flows to Class.forName()
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/ff4j-rest-api-class-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

// Find all Class.forName calls and trace back to PropertyApiBean or FlippingStrategyApiBean
from MethodCall forNameCall, Method enclosingMethod
where
  forNameCall.getMethod().hasName("forName") and
  forNameCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") and
  enclosingMethod = forNameCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
  (
    enclosingMethod.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.ff4j.property.util", "PropertyFactory") or
    enclosingMethod.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.ff4j.utils", "MappingUtil") or
    enclosingMethod.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.ff4j.property", "PropertyClass") or
    enclosingMethod.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.ff4j.web.embedded", "ConsoleOperations")
  )
select forNameCall,
  "Class.forName() called in " + enclosingMethod.getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + enclosingMethod.getName() +
  " - the class name argument may be user-controlled via REST API or web console parameters."
findings.hypothesis: Multiple REST API and web console endpoints pass user-controlled class names to Class.forName()

message.text: Class.forName() called in PropertyClass.fromString - the class name argument may be user-controlled via REST API or web console parameters.
Class.forName() called in PropertyFactory.createProperty - the class name argument may be user-controlled via REST API or web console parameters.
Class.forName() called in MappingUtil.instanceFlippingStrategy - the class name argument may be user-controlled via REST API or web console parameters.
Class.forName() called in ConsoleOperations.createFeature - the class name argument may be user-controlled via REST API or web console parameters.
Class.forName() called in ConsoleOperations.updateFlippingStrategy - the class name argument may be user-controlled via REST API or web console parameters.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name PropertyFactory and MappingUtil newInstance() calls after forName()
 * @description Confirms the complete exploit chain: forName() + newInstance() in PropertyFactory and MappingUtil
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/ff4j-forname-newinstance-full-chain
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

from MethodCall newInstanceCall, MethodCall forNameCall
where
  forNameCall.getMethod().hasName("forName") and
  forNameCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") and
  forNameCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName().matches("org.ff4j%") and
  newInstanceCall.getMethod().hasName("newInstance") and
  newInstanceCall.getEnclosingCallable() = forNameCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
  (
    DataFlow::localFlow(DataFlow::exprNode(forNameCall), DataFlow::exprNode(newInstanceCall.getQualifier()))
    or
    exists(MethodCall intermediate |
      (intermediate.getMethod().hasName("getConstructor") or
       intermediate.getMethod().hasName("getDeclaredConstructor")) and
      intermediate.getEnclosingCallable() = forNameCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
      DataFlow::localFlow(DataFlow::exprNode(forNameCall), DataFlow::exprNode(intermediate.getQualifier())) and
      DataFlow::localFlow(DataFlow::exprNode(intermediate), DataFlow::exprNode(newInstanceCall.getQualifier()))
    )
  )
select newInstanceCall,
  "Complete exploit chain: Class.forName() at $@ followed by newInstance() in " +
  forNameCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." +
  forNameCall.getEnclosingCallable().getName() +
  " - user-controlled class name enables arbitrary class instantiation.",
  forNameCall, "Class.forName() sink"
findings.hypothesis: Complete exploit chain confirmation: forName() + newInstance() in ff4j classes with user-controlled input

message.text: Complete exploit chain: Class.forName() at [Class.forName() sink](1) followed by newInstance() in PropertiesParser.parseProperties - user-controlled class name enables arbitrary class instantiation.
Complete exploit chain: Class.forName() at [Class.forName() sink](1) followed by newInstance() in PropertiesParser.parseFeatures - user-controlled class name enables arbitrary class instantiation.

Complete exploit chain: Class.forName() at [Class.forName() sink](1) followed by newInstance() in PropertyFactory.createProperty - user-controlled class name enables arbitrary class instantiation.

Complete exploit chain: Class.forName() at [Class.forName() sink](1) followed by newInstance() in MappingUtil.instanceFlippingStrategy - user-controlled class name enables arbitrary class instantiation.
Complete exploit chain: Class.forName() at [Class.forName() sink](1) followed by newInstance() in ConsoleOperations.createFeature - user-controlled class name enables arbitrary class instantiation.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ConsoleServlet file upload processes ff4j config with Class.forName
 * @description ConsoleServlet processes uploaded config files, which triggers Class.forName() on class names
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/ff4j-console-upload-class-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

// Show entry points in ConsoleServlet/ConsoleOperations that process uploads
// and the Class.forName calls they lead to
from MethodCall fileUploadRelatedCall
where
  fileUploadRelatedCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.ff4j.web.embedded", "ConsoleServlet") and
  (
    fileUploadRelatedCall.getMethod().hasName("parseRequest") or
    fileUploadRelatedCall.getMethod().hasName("getFileContents") or
    fileUploadRelatedCall.getMethod().hasName("importFile") or
    fileUploadRelatedCall.getMethod().getName().toLowerCase().matches("%upload%") or
    fileUploadRelatedCall.getMethod().getName().toLowerCase().matches("%import%") or
    fileUploadRelatedCall.getMethod().getName().toLowerCase().matches("%parse%")
  )
select fileUploadRelatedCall,
  "File processing call in ConsoleServlet." + fileUploadRelatedCall.getEnclosingCallable().getName() +
  " - uploaded XML/YAML config files are parsed with Class.forName() on class names from the file."
findings.hypothesis: ConsoleServlet file upload handling processes user-submitted config files triggering Class.forName()

message.text: File processing call in ConsoleServlet.doPost - uploaded XML/YAML config files are parsed with Class.forName() on class names from the file.
File processing call in ConsoleServlet.doPost - uploaded XML/YAML config files are parsed with Class.forName() on class names from the file.
File processing call in ConsoleServlet.doPost - uploaded XML/YAML config files are parsed with Class.forName() on class names from the file.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name PropertyFactory uses Class.forName with potentially untrusted class names
 * @description ff4j PropertyFactory uses Class.forName to instantiate property types from class names
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/ff4j-property-factory-class-loading
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName().matches("org.ff4j%")
select mc, "Class.forName() call in ff4j code - check if class name can be user-controlled: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName()
findings.hypothesis: ff4j's PropertyFactory and related classes use Class.forName() which may accept user-controlled class names

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name User input written directly to HTTP response (XSS)
 * @description User-controlled data written to HTTP response without encoding
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/ff4j-xss-response-write
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module XSSConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (mc.getMethod().hasName("print") or mc.getMethod().hasName("println") or mc.getMethod().hasName("write")) and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "Writer") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (mc.getMethod().hasName("print") or mc.getMethod().hasName("println")) and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("javax.servlet", "ServletResponse") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
  }
}

module XSSFlow = TaintTracking::Global<XSSConfig>;
import XSSFlow::PathGraph

from XSSFlow::PathNode source, XSSFlow::PathNode sink
where XSSFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "User input written to HTTP response without encoding from $@.", source.getNode(), "user input"
findings.hypothesis: XSS: user input flows directly to HTTP response writer without encoding in web console

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Path traversal in static resource controller
 * @description User-controlled path segment used to serve files without path validation
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/ff4j-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module PathTraversalConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr ce |
      ce.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = ce.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getResourceAsStream") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getArgument(0)
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getResource") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getArgument(0)
    )
  }
}

module PathTraversalFlow = TaintTracking::Global<PathTraversalConfig>;
import PathTraversalFlow::PathGraph

from PathTraversalFlow::PathNode source, PathTraversalFlow::PathNode sink
where PathTraversalFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "User input flows to file path operation from $@.", source.getNode(), "user input"
findings.hypothesis: Path traversal in static resource controller using user-controlled path input
